question_title: Will Romuald Wadagni win the Benin presidential election in April 2026?
summary: Wadagni is the overwhelming favorite to win Benin's April 12, 2026 presidential election. He has full backing of the ruling coalition that controls all 109 parliamentary seats, faces only one minor opposition candidate (Paul Hounkpè from FCBE, whose ticket got ~11% in 2021), and the main opposition party Les Démocrates was excluded from the race. In 2021, Talon won 86% in a similarly restricted field. The only realistic NO paths are election disruption (another coup attempt, despite ECOWAS security support), postponement beyond April 30, or some unforeseen crisis. These tail risks collectively warrant ~7% probability of No.

factors.description: Ruling coalition controls ALL 109 parliamentary seats and has full state apparatus support
Only two candidates approved - main opposition excluded, leaving only moderate/minor challenger
2021 precedent: Talon won 86% in similar restricted field; Hounkpè's ticket got ~11%
Opposition leader Boni Yayi resigned from politics; Les Démocrates in 'clinical death'
Multiple credible sources describe Wadagni as 'overwhelming favorite' and 'runaway favourite'
December 2025 coup attempt creates residual instability risk for election disruption
Low voter turnout (36.7% in Jan elections) could complicate legitimacy but unlikely to change outcome
ECOWAS security training and support reduces disruption risk

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.93 (93% probability YES)**

Key reasoning:
- **Wadagni is the dominant candidate** backed by Talon's ruling coalition, which swept all 109 parliamentary seats in January 2026
- **The main opposition was excluded** from the ballot; the sole remaining challenger (Paul Hounkpè/FCBE) is a minor figure whose ticket received only ~11% in 2021
- **Historical precedent**: Talon won 86% in a similarly restricted 2021 election
- **~7% NO probability** accounts for tail risks: another coup attempt (~2-3%), postponement past April 30 (~1-2%), and unforeseen events (~1-2%)
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will Romuald Wadagni win the Benin presidential election in April 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42830)

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve as Yes if Romuald Wadagni is officially declared the winner of the April 2026 Benin presidential election by the Constitutional Court of Benin on or before April 30, 2026.
fine_print:
If the election is not held or the results are postponed beyond April 30, 2026, the question will resolve as No.
